lessons.title: What is Electricity?
lessons.content: Electricity is the flow of electric charge, usually carried by electrons moving through a conductor.
lessons.prediction: What happens if the circuit has a gap?

lessons.title: How Does a Battery Work?
lessons.content: A battery stores chemical energy and converts it into electrical energy through a redox reaction between two electrodes.
lessons.prediction: What happens if you connect the battery backwards?

quiz.q: A battery converts chemical energy into?

lessons.title: What is Voltage?
lessons.content: Voltage is the electrical potential difference that pushes current through a circuit, like pressure in a pipe.
lessons.prediction: What happens if voltage is too high for a component?

quiz.q: Voltage is similar to what in a water pipe analogy?

lessons.title: How CPUs Work
lessons.content: The CPU executes instructions using cores, clock cycles, and cache memory to process data quickly.
lessons.prediction: What happens if you add more cores but not enough cooling?

lessons.title: How RAM Works
lessons.content: RAM temporarily stores active data so the CPU can access it instantly, unlike slower long-term storage.
lessons.prediction: What happens if RAM is too small for the OS?

lessons.title: Charging and Heat
lessons.content: Faster charging pushes more current into the battery, which increases heat generation.
lessons.prediction: What happens if you fast-charge a battery with poor cooling?
